title: Storing Data with Light in 3D
summary: Scientists have found a cool new way to store lots of data using light in three dimensions. They use three parts of light: amplitude, phase, and polarization, which helps fit more information into the same space. This is like writing on a 3D block instead of a flat paper, so it can hold more data and make it faster to save and read. The researchers used a special computer program called a neural network to help read the data from light patterns. This could help make data centers smaller and keep information safer. It's a big step for storing all the data we use every day, like photos and videos.

keywords.explanation: a form of energy that we can see and use to carry information
three-dimensional, meaning something has height, width, and depth like a cube
information stored on computers, like pictures or text
a smart computer program that learns patterns to solve problems
a place to keep data so you can use it later

background_read: Data storage is how we save information on devices like phones and computers. As we use more data for things like videos and games, we need better ways to store it without taking up too much space. Light is used in technologies like DVDs to store data, but this new method uses light in 3D to pack more data into small areas, which could help with the growing need for storage in our digital world.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists and researchers led by Xiaodi Tan from Fujian Normal University
WHAT: Developed a new holographic data storage method using light in 3D to store more data
WHERE: Research published in Optica journal, with potential global use
WHY: To meet the growing demand for data storage by making it more efficient and compact

perspectives.description: This method shows how combining light properties with AI can lead to better data storage solutions.
